# Matrix on VS Code – AI Guide

- **Purpose & entrypoint**: VS Code extension surfacing Matrix courses/assignments. Activation lives in [src/extension.ts](src/extension.ts); registers commands (signin/signout/refresh/preview) and creates tree view `matrixExplorerView` under container `matrixView` defined in package contributions.
- **Auth flow**: Primary path is cookie login via [src/MatrixManager.ts](src/MatrixManager.ts) + [src/services/AuthService.ts](src/services/AuthService.ts); credentials path is marked deprecated. `MatrixManager.signIn()` asks UI for cookie, calls `AuthService.loginWithCookie()` (`GET /api/users/login` with Cookie header), then persists cookie + user status in [src/globalState.ts](src/globalState.ts). `signOut()` clears state and shows info message. Keep using `globalState.getCookie()` when calling APIs.
- **Global state**: `globalState.initialize(context)` in activation stores VS Code `Memento`. Cookie key `matrix_cookie`, user status key `matrix_user_status`; `clear()` resets both and marks signed out. Prefer reading via getters instead of reading raw state.
- **Tree data**: [src/sidebar/MatrixTreeDataProvider.ts](src/sidebar/MatrixTreeDataProvider.ts) supplies nodes defined in [src/sidebar/MatrixNode.ts](src/sidebar/MatrixNode.ts). Root shows ongoing/finished categories. Course nodes expand to pending/completed assignment groups; `AssignmentNode` executes `matrix-on-vscode.previewProblem`. Refresh logic: `refresh({ force: true })` clears cached courses/assignments; `refreshAssignments(courseId?)` invalidates per-course cache. Loading/error/info nodes use `InfoNode` with icons/commands.
- **Course API usage**: [src/services/CourseService.ts](src/services/CourseService.ts) hits `https://matrix.sysu.edu.cn` by default (pass custom baseUrl for staging). Requests require Cookie header. Responses may be plain JSON or `{ type, body }` encoded; `decodeBody()` in [src/util/body-encode.ts](src/util/body-encode.ts) supports `aes-256-gcm` and `base64` via Web Crypto + js-base64. Service normalizes diverse payload shapes for courses/assignments (many alias fields) and determines `isFinished`/`isFullScore`/ongoing status heuristically; reuse this service instead of parsing API responses ad hoc. Handles `未登录` errors by prompting sign-out.
- **Assignment detail & preview**: `previewAssignment()` in [src/webview/assignmentPreview.ts](src/webview/assignmentPreview.ts) fetches detail via `CourseService.fetchAssignmentDetail()`, builds markdown, renders through `markdown.api.render`, and shows one cached webview panel per courseId:assignmentId. Attachments render as links or code blocks; fallback HTML escapes markdown if render fails.
- **Models & constants**: Shared types exported from [src/shared.ts](src/shared.ts) combine [src/models/Assignment.ts](src/models/Assignment.ts) and [src/models/Course.ts](src/models/Course.ts). View/context ids and labels live in [src/constants.ts](src/constants.ts); keep context values consistent with `package.json` contributions when adding nodes/commands.
- **Build & bundling**: Uses esbuild via [esbuild.js](esbuild.js). `pnpm compile` runs type-check + lint + build. `pnpm package` adds `--production`. Type check `pnpm check-types`; lint `pnpm lint` (eslint on src). Watch development: `pnpm watch` (runs `watch:tsc` and `watch:esbuild`); VS Code tasks `watch`, `npm: watch:tsc`, `npm: watch:esbuild`, and `watch-tests` are available. Tests via `pnpm test` (runs vscode-test) after `pretest` builds/lints.
- **API dependencies**: Network via node-fetch v2; avoid fetch v3 APIs. AES helpers rely on global Web Crypto available in Node 20+. External constants for base URLs at [src/constants.ts](src/constants.ts).
- **UI patterns**: Use `vscode.window.withProgress` around network calls, `ThemeIcon` for tree nodes, and quick-pick/input prompts from [src/services/UIService.ts](src/services/UIService.ts). Handle expired cookies by calling `matrixManager.signOut()` and triggering tree refresh.
- **Extending features**: When adding commands/views, update `contributes` in [package.json](package.json) and wire registration in [src/extension.ts](src/extension.ts). For API calls, favor `CourseService`/`AuthService` to keep normalization and error handling consistent. Keep tree updates via provider `refresh` methods to maintain caches and UI state.
